title: cerefolium anthriscus
intro: Cerefolium anthriscus, also known as wild chervil or garden chervil, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of cerefolium anthriscus include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds like coumarins and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia due to its calming effects. Cerefolium anthriscus is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
